name: K8s Pod Health Summary
description: Audit pod health across a Kubernetes namespace. Calls the live openagentic_kubernetes MCP, flags pods that are not Running or have crash-looped more than 5 times, asks the platform LLM to write a 3-bullet operator summary, strips any chain-of-thought preamble, and renders an HTML report via webhook_response.

meta.purpose: Audit pod health in a Kubernetes namespace and produce a 3-bullet operator-facing summary plus an HTML report listing unhealthy pods.
how_it_works: 1. Lists every pod in the chosen namespace via openagentic_kubernetes.k8s_list_pods (live cluster, not cached).
2. Flags any pod that is not Running or has restart count > 5 — these are the 'unhealthy' rows.
3. Computes total pod count + namespace label in a single analyze transform.
4. The platform LLM writes a 3-bullet operator narrative naming the worst offenders.
5. A CoT-stripper transform removes any 'Here is...' / 'The user wants...' preamble so the output is presentation-ready.
6. webhook_response renders the final HTML report with operator summary on top and the raw unhealthy-pods JSON underneath.
meta.expected_output: HTML report containing a 3-bullet operator summary at the top and the raw unhealthy-pods JSON array underneath. Status code 200, Content-Type text/html.
meta.useful_when: Daily morning health check, pre-deploy sanity check, or when oncall pages about a noisy namespace and you need a one-shot 'is everything OK?' answer.

operations.value: {{ (() => { let pods = []; if (Array.isArray(input.pods)) { pods = input.pods; } else if (typeof input.content === 'string') { try { const parsed = JSON.parse(input.content); if (Array.isArray(parsed.pods)) pods = parsed.pods; } catch (e) {} } return pods.filter(p => p && (p.status !== 'Running' || (p.restarts || 0) > 5)); })() }}

{{ (() => { if (typeof input.namespace === 'string') return input.namespace; if (typeof input.content === 'string') { try { return JSON.parse(input.content).namespace || 'unknown'; } catch (e) {} } return 'unknown'; })() }}

{{ (() => { if (Array.isArray(input.pods)) return input.pods.length; if (typeof input.content === 'string') { try { const p = JSON.parse(input.content).pods; return Array.isArray(p) ? p.length : 0; } catch (e) { return 0; } } return 0; })() }}

data.label: Summarize Findings
data.prompt:
Operator-facing 3-bullet summary of unhealthy Kubernetes pods in namespace {{input.namespace_label}}.

Unhealthy pods (JSON array, may be empty):
{{input.unhealthy}}

Total pods in namespace: {{input.pod_count}}.

Output rules (MUST follow):
1. Start the response with a single `-` character — no preamble, no "Here is", no "The user wants", no thinking out loud.
2. Exactly 3 short bullets, each on its own line, each beginning with `-`.
3. Reference pod names verbatim when applicable.
4. If the unhealthy array is empty, state that all pods are healthy and recommend a routine future check.

operations.value: {{ (() => { const raw = String(input.content || ''); const lines = raw.split(/\r?\n/); let firstBulletIdx = -1; for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) { const t = lines[i].trim(); if (t.startsWith('-') || t.startsWith('•') || t.startsWith('*')) { firstBulletIdx = i; break; } } if (firstBulletIdx === -1) { return raw.trim(); } return lines.slice(firstBulletIdx).join('\n').trim(); })() }}

data.label: HTML Report
data.bodyTemplate:
K8s Pod Health Report — namespace {{steps.filter.namespace_label}}

Operator Summary

{{steps.clean_summary.clean_content}}

Unhealthy Pods (raw)

{{steps.filter.unhealthy}}
data.artifactDescription: Pod health audit produced by the k8s-pod-health-summary template.
